articles.headline: Industry says proposed NASA changes to commercial space station plans create confusion
articles.blurb: NASA’s proposed changes to its support of commercial space stations have created concern and confusion among companies developing them, the head of an industry organization warned.
articles.takeaway: NASA's ambiguous transition timeline for commercial space station support risks delaying private sector investments critical to maintaining continuous U.S. orbital capabilities post-ISS.

articles.headline: Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk
articles.blurb: Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).
articles.takeaway: Effective SRM requires organizations to systematically justify why residual risks remain acceptable after implementing feasible controls, moving beyond theoretical analysis to documented risk acceptance decisions.

articles.headline: The Evolution of Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.
articles.takeaway: Modern SMS transforms aviation safety from incident-driven responses to systematic risk prediction, reducing accidents before they occur rather than learning from them.

articles.headline: SBQuantum and Spire to send quantum diamond magnetometer into orbit

articles.headline: ispace redesigns lunar lander, introduces lunar communications service

articles.headline: Satellite Spots a Spawn

articles.headline: Here’s How Much Range The Airbus A350-1000 Has Compared To The Boeing 777-300ER
articles.blurb: The Airbus A350-1000 and Boeing 777-300ER are long-haul aviation titans, but the A350's slight edge in range may not be as decisive as it seems.
articles.takeaway: The A350-1000's marginal range advantage becomes negligible when operators factor in payload-range tradeoffs and real-world fuel planning, making aircraft selection dependent on route economics rather than specifications alone.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization through technology integration offers the most immediate pathway to accommodate projected traffic growth while reducing aviation's environmental footprint before new aircraft designs reach widespread adoption.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: U.S. GSSAP satellites execute GEO handoff to monitor China’s Shijian-29 spacecraft

articles.headline: Government use of commercial procurement models has limitations in space
articles.blurb: While government agencies are increasingly turning to commercial contracting mechanisms for space capabilities, that does not necessarily translate to additional business opportunities for the companies offering them.
articles.takeaway: Government space procurement shifts toward commercial models, yet agencies' strict requirements and limited volume may not expand actual contract opportunities despite modernized purchasing frameworks.

articles.headline: Station Nation: Aaron Rose, Cold Stowage Mission Manager
